title: Reading and Use of English - Part 7
description: You are going to read an article about four people sharing their personal relationship with fashion and clothing. For questions 43–52, choose from the people (A–D). The people may be chosen more than once.

texts.A:
### Julia, 26, Marketing Assistant
I’ve always enjoyed experimenting with clothes. [43]For me, fashion is a way to express my mood and creativity[/43]. I don’t follow trends strictly, but [47]I like to add my own twist to popular styles[/47]. Shopping with friends is fun, but [50]I prefer to choose outfits that reflect who I am, not just what’s in magazines[/50].
texts.C:
### Ben, 34, Teacher
I used to think fashion was superficial, but [44]I’ve realised that feeling comfortable in what I wear boosts my confidence[/44]. I don’t spend much on clothes, but [48]I make sure what I buy is good quality and lasts a long time[/48]. [51]I also try to buy from ethical brands when possible[/51].
texts.D:
### Sara, 21, Student
[45]Growing up, I didn’t have much money for new clothes, so I learned to be creative with what I had[/45]. Now, I enjoy finding bargains in second-hand shops. [49]I love the thrill of discovering unique pieces[/49]. [52]Fashion for me is about individuality, not impressing others[/52].
texts.B:
### Omar, 29, Chef
My job means I spend a lot of time in uniform, so [46]when I’m off work, I like to wear bright, unusual clothes[/46]. [47]People sometimes comment on my bold choices, but I don’t mind[/47]. [49]I think clothes should be fun and make you feel good[/49].

questions.question: sees fashion as a form of self-expression?
questions.explanation: Julia says fashion is a way to express her mood and creativity.

questions.question: has found that clothing can affect their self-confidence?
questions.explanation: Ben realised feeling comfortable in clothes boosts his confidence.

questions.question: became resourceful with clothes due to limited finances?
questions.explanation: Sara learned to be creative with clothes because she didn’t have much money.

questions.question: enjoys wearing colourful clothes outside of work?
questions.explanation: Omar likes to wear bright, unusual clothes when not in uniform.

questions.question: likes to put a personal spin on current trends?
questions.explanation: Julia adds her own twist to popular styles.

questions.question: prefers to buy fewer, higher-quality clothes?
questions.explanation: Ben makes sure to buy good quality clothes that last.

questions.question: enjoys finding distinctive items of clothing?
questions.explanation: Sara loves discovering unique pieces in second-hand shops.

questions.question: chooses clothes based on personal taste rather than trends?
questions.explanation: Julia prefers outfits that reflect who she is, not just what’s in magazines.

questions.question: tries to support ethical clothing brands?
questions.explanation: Ben tries to buy from ethical brands when possible.

questions.question: believes fashion is about being yourself, not impressing others?
questions.explanation: Sara says fashion is about individuality, not impressing others.
